page: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and instruction-level performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside a top-tier rating in Geekbench ray tracing. Multi-core performance is strong in stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core evaluations, as well as in software compilation with Clang and image processing. However, single-core performance remains in the average category across stress-ng, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks demonstrate strong throughput, though the overall Passmark memory score is average.

Given the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the lack of local storage, this instance is designed for workloads that scale across many cores and require high network throughput. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, where it demonstrates strong benchmark performance. While it can handle small to medium LLM prompt processing with strong efficiency, text generation speeds for larger models fall into the average tier. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, combined with 200 Gbps network bandwidth, offers a consolidated platform for network-heavy and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) via Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Passmark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core capabilities in software compilation and image processing. Conversely, single-core performance and text generation for large language models remain in the average tier. This resource profile is suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, database operations, and high-throughput network tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and encryption benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-core x86_64 platform featuring 512 GB RAM and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded and network-heavy enterprise workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Top-tier performance in CPU encryption, extended instructions, and ray tracing benchmarks
Strong multi-core capabilities for software compilation and database operations
Average single-core and large-model LLM text generation performance
